heading: 2024 Releases
body:
- Allows you to [Apply Changes](scale-cluster-payment) and sends
  you an email with a cluster link so that you can return to the cluster
  to continue making changes.

- Supports configuring [custom ciphers](set-custom-cipher-suite-configuration)
  for conducting all node-to-node and client-to-client communications in
  your Atlas clusters.

- Improves handling of Backup Compliance Policy where disabling Backup Compliance Policy becomes a two-person
  process that requires approvals by MongoDB Support. To learn more, see
  [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy).

- Adds a message asking you to increase the oplog size on the source and
  destination clusters to prevent errors during the [verify your data during live migrations to Atlas](verify-migrations)
  of live migration. To learn more, see [Oplog Requirements](oplog-reqs-live-migration).

- Adds a spill to disk during sort Atlas UI performance metric
  and a `Spill to disk during sort is` [sort alert condition](alert-conditions-sort)
  that reflect the total number of writes to disk caused by [$sort](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/sort) stages.
  This metric is also added to the [Datadog](datadog-integration).
  To learn more, see [Review Available Metrics](review-available-metrics)
  and search for Sort.

* Supports [Flex clusters](flex-migration).

* Allows you to [verify your data during live migrations to Atlas](verify-migrations).

* Updates the [createThirdPartyIntegration](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupintegration)
  API resource for the [Datadog](datadog-integration) with
  `sendCollectionLatencyMetrics` and `sendDatabaseMetrics` options
  for sending data from your Atlas cluster to Datadog.

* Sends the `availability_zone`, `provider`, and `region` replica set
  tags as part of the metrics payload to Prometheus. Adding these tags
  allows you to aggregate data by region, provider, and availability zone.
  To learn more, see [Return the Latest Targets for Prometheus](prometheus-discovery-endpoint).

* Supports filtering the results in the [Performance Advisor](performance-advisor)
  by shards and hosts, namespace, time range, and date and time.

- Adds [checkMetadataConsistency](manual/reference/privilege-actions) to the list of supported
  privilege actions for the Atlas administrators under the
  [built-in role](manual/reference/built-in-roles).

- Starting from October 31, 2024, stops support for MongoDB 5.0,
  which is EOL. To learn more, see [What happens to Atlas clusters using a MongoDB version nearing end of life?](atlas-eol-upgrade)

* Supports filtering the results in the [Query Profiler](query-profiler) by hosts.

- Supports MongoDB 8.0 and upgrades `M0` and `M2/M5` clusters
  to MongoDB 8.0. To learn more, see [MongoDB 8.0 Release Notes](manual/release-notes/8.0).

- Adds an option to specify a designated Atlas [security contact](organization-settings)
  to receive security-related notifications.

- Improves [Cluster Auto-Scaling](cluster-autoscaling) for
  Atlas clusters to allow you to scale Atlas clusters up
  to 50% faster, respond to resource demands 5X faster in real-time,
  and optimize performance, while reducing operational costs.

- For Atlas clusters deployed on AWS (Amazon Web Services) and Azure (Microsoft Azure), allows you to
  download the snapshot over the private endpoints in the same region as
  the snapshot. To learn more, see [Restore from a Locally-Downloaded Snapshot](restore-from-local-file).

- Adds the `PAGES_REQUESTED_FROM_CACHE` and the `DISK_QUEUE_DEPTH` metrics
  to the list of metrics you can send from your Atlas cluster to Datadog.
  To learn more, see [Datadog](datadog-integration).

- Adds the `ttldeleted` metric to the `Opcounters` hardware metric.
  To learn more, [Review Available Metrics](review-available-metrics)
  and search for `ttldeleted`.

* Adds `M10` cluster tier support for the [Azure](microsoft-azure-supported-regions)
  `SWEDEN_CENTRAL` and `SWEDEN_SOUTH` regions.

- Adds the Disk Throughput read and write [Review Available Metrics](review-available-metrics).

- Adds the Cache Ratio [Review Available Metrics](review-available-metrics).
  A high cache fill ratio indicates that most data requests are being served
  from memory, leading to faster query performance and reduced disk I/O.

- Sends an email if your live migration process is successful,
  pending 12 hours after the cutover, or has failed.

- Adds a [Private Preview program](https://www.mongodb.com/products/platform/atlas-online-archive#promo) for [Online Archives](online-archive-overview) for Atlas clusters deployed on Google Cloud.

- Supports using a customer-managed key (CMK) from Azure Key Vault (AKV)
  to further encrypt your data at rest in Atlas. To learn more,
  see [Manage Customer Keys with Azure Key Vault Over Private Endpoints](security-azure-kms-pvt-endpoint).

* Supports Azure (Microsoft Azure) Extended Standard IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) and Extended Storage to limited regions.
  For Low CPU or General clusters deployed on
  Azure (Microsoft Azure) in one of the [regions that support extended storage](microsoft-azure-storage-supported-regions),
  you can extend IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) and the data volumes' storage capacity in the Atlas UI.
  To learn more, see [Extend Storage Capacity and IOPS on Azure](change-storage-capacity-azure).

- Supports cluster snapshots on Azure (Microsoft Azure). To learn more, see [export your cloud backup snapshots](cloud-provider-snapshot-export).

- Converts auto-scaling notifications into configurable alerts. To learn
  more, see [Configure Alerts for Auto-scaling Events](custom-alerts-auto-scaling-events).

* Allows you to [export your cloud backup snapshots](cloud-provider-snapshot-export) to an Azure Blob Storage Container.

* Provides efficient [cross-project restores](restore-time-optimizations) for clusters that have AWS (Amazon Web Services) nodes
  created before March 27, 2024.

* Allows you to enable faster restores for AWS (Amazon Web Services).

- Adds resource tags [Tags on Projects](project-tags) to customer
  invoice CSV (Comma-Separated Values) exports and invoice API (Application Programming Interface) responses.

* Increases shard limit for an Atlas cluster from 50 to 70.

* Adds the ability to unlink organizations from your paying organization from
  the Atlas UI. To learn more, see [Unlink Organizations](unlink-with-cross-org-billing).

* Removes support for legacy two-factor authentication.
  Use [enable multi-factor authentication](atlas-enable-mfa) instead.

- Introduces the general availability of OIDC (OpenID Connect) [Workforce](oidc-authentication-workforce)
  and [Workload Identity Federation (Applications)](oidc-authentication-workload).

* Adds the Migration Hub to Atlas. The Migration Hub displays
  available migration resources and the status of migrations in progress.
  To learn more, see [Monitor Migrations](monitor-migrations).

* Allows you to add low carbon regions and shows Low Carbon indicators
  on AWS (Amazon Web Services) and Google Cloud regions when you create a cluster in the Atlas UI.

* Allows you to [monitor collection-level query latency](namespace-insights)
  in a new Query Insights tab in the Atlas UI.
  Atlas supports this metric for `M10+` dedicated clusters.

* When you upgrade a replica set to a multi-sharded cluster, requires
  that you upgrade to a **single** shard cluster first, by restarting
  your application, **reconnecting** to the cluster, and then adding
  additional shards. To learn more, see [Scale your Replica Set to a Sharded Cluster](scale-cluster-sharding).

- Adds disk, memory, and CPU utilization metrics to the sharded cluster metrics
  page UI view.

- Disables a load balancer on source clusters during live migration.
  To learn more, see [balancers in pull live migration](lm-pull-load-balancers) and
  [balancers in push live migration](lm-push-load-balancers).

* Allows you to specify `yearly` option in the [cluster-level backup policy](configure-backup-policy).

* Allows you to use the Atlas UI to [add team members](edit-team-members)
  that are part of the organization or users that previously received an
  invitation to join the organization.

- Supports archiving data using Online Archive to Azure (Microsoft Azure) storage
  for Atlas clusters deployed on Azure (Microsoft Azure). To learn more,
  see [Online Archive](config-online-archive).

* Introduces [Atlas Stream Processing](atlas-sp-overview) in
  public preview. With Atlas Stream Processing, you can process streaming data in
  Atlas.

* Supports Atlas deployments in the following new
  [cloud provider regions](cloud-providers-list):

  - AWS (Amazon Web Services)

    - Israel (`il-central-1`)

    - Canada West (`ca-west-1`)

  - Azure (Microsoft Azure)

    - Poland (`polandcentral`)

    - Israel Central (`israelcenttral`)

    - Italy North (`italynorth`)

  - Google Cloud

    - Berlin, Germany (`europe-west10`)

* Supports adding resource tags to projects in Atlas. To learn
  more, see [Tags on Projects](project-tags).

* Fixes an issue where Atlas inaccurately reported the
  network bytes out metric that appears in the
  System Network chart. This release resets this metric and
  the previous values no longer appear. To learn more, see
  [Review Available Metrics](review-available-metrics) and [System Network Out is](<System Network Out is>).

- Supports MongoDB 7.2.

- Supports Google Cloud for Atlas oplog store. To learn more, see
  [Continuous Cloud Backup](pit-restore).

* Supports replica set to sharded cluster migrations on MongoDB
  6.0.8+ with Atlas Live Migration (pull). To learn more, see
  [Live Migrate a MongoDB 6.0.5 or Later Cluster into Atlas](c2c-pull-live-migration).

* Supports cluster node disk pre-warming. To learn more, see
  [Enable or Disable Fast Disk Pre-Warming](disk-pre-warming).
